mechanism_narrative: A falling federal funds rate transmits easier financial conditions, lowers equity discount rates, and supports higher S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is strongest when the policy rate is already above the recent inflation rate, because cuts then represent genuine easing rather than a shallow adjustment.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines over the prior 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS cuts are followed by negative 20d SPX returns more often than positive returns over triggered instances, or if the signal loses directional accuracy in a rising-inflation regime with CPIAUCSL accelerating year over year.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate identifies weakening labor income and deteriorating household demand, which should pressure earnings expectations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect is most informative when unemployment rises faster than payroll growth decelerates, confirming broad labor slack rather than measurement noise.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered unemployment spikes are followed by positive 20d SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the relationship disappears when PAYEMS is stable or rising.

mechanism_narrative: A broad retail-sales slowdown signals weaker final demand and lower near-term revenue growth for large-cap firms, which should reduce S&P 500 returns over the next week as analysts revise sales expectations downward. The effect is strongest when sales are contracting while housing activity is not improving, indicating demand softness is not offset by a domestic housing impulse.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth turns negative versus the prior month
failure_condition: Reject if negative RSAFS growth is not followed by below-median 5d SPX returns, or if the signal remains negative even when HOUST is accelerating strongly enough to offset demand weakness.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates stronger current output and better cyclical earnings momentum, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal should fail when production gains are driven by inventory restocking without accompanying labor improvement, because then the output impulse does not translate into cash-flow growth.
failure_condition: Reject if sustained INDPRO uptrends are not associated with positive 20d SPX returns over a rolling 3-year window, or if the signal turns negative when PAYEMS is also weakening.

mechanism_narrative: A collapse in housing starts together with weak retail sales identifies a synchronized domestic demand slowdown that should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. Housing weakness matters most when it coincides with falling sales because the combination implies both durable-goods demand and household spending are deteriorating.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 6-month moving average and RSAFS growth is negative
failure_condition: Reject if the joint HOUST-RSAFS weakness does not precede negative 60d SPX returns more often than positive returns, or if either series alone explains the effect without the interaction.

mechanism_narrative: An inflation surprise above the recent trend combined with an already positive policy rate signals tightening real financial conditions, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism fails when inflation rises but the Fed is still below neutral and markets interpret the move as growth-positive rather than restrictive.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates while FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.5%
failure_condition: Reject if high-inflation/high-rate episodes are not followed by below-baseline 20d SPX returns, or if the sign flips positive when CPIAUCSL accelerates but FEDFUNDS is unchanged.

mechanism_narrative: A payrolls rebound after a prior weakness period signals labor-market stabilization and should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days through improved earnings confidence and risk appetite. The signal is strongest in post-shock recovery periods, when investors reprice a lower probability of recession.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises for 2 consecutive months after a prior decline
failure_condition: Reject if payroll rebound episodes are not followed by above-median 5d SPX returns, or if the signal fails when UNRATE is flat or falling.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production combined with a falling unemployment rate indicates a broad cyclical upswing that should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism is strongest when both real activity and labor utilization improve together, because then earnings upgrades are more durable than a single-series bounce.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO is above its 6-month average and UNRATE is falling over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint INDPRO/UNRATE improvement does not precede positive 60d SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the effect is absent when only one proxy improves.
